source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Paconianus, Sex. (Sextus) d. 35; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Paconianus's poem survives in a single fragment, indicating his minor status in Latin poetry.

title: De Verborum Significatione
author: Sex. Pompeius Festus
classification_notes: Festus's 'De Verborum Significatione' is an indispensable Latin lexicon, abridging Verrius Flaccus's earlier work. As the largest surviving ancient Latin dictionary, it is a key resource for Latin lexicography, etymology, and Roman antiquities, regularly consulted by scholars.

title: Liber Regularum, fragmentum
author: Sex. Pomponius
classification_notes: A fragment of Sextus Pomponius's Liber Regularum, a legal treatise. Only 21 words survive, providing limited insight into Roman juristic literature.

title: palliatae
author: Sex. Turpilius
classification_notes: The surviving fragments of Turpilius' palliatae (Roman comedies in Greek dress) are sparse and preserved by later grammarians. They provide limited insight into Republican comic drama and are of interest only to specialists in Latin comedy.

title: carmen
author: Sextilius Ena
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Ena, Sextilius; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: A six-word poetic fragment (carmen) attributed to the obscure poet Sextilius Ena. The brevity of the text precludes further literary assessment, but it is preserved in the Morel collection of early Latin fragments.

title: Elegiae
author: Sextus Propertius
classification_notes: The Elegies of Sextus Propertius are a cornerstone of Augustan elegy. Across four books, Propertius explores love, loss, and Roman identity, with his beloved Cynthia as the central figure. His dense, learned, and passionate poetry has been a major influence on the Western lyric tradition. Widely studied in Latin courses and by scholars, the Elegiae is a canonical text of Latin literature.

title: De Condicionibus Agrorum
author: Siculus Flaccus
classification_notes: Siculus Flaccus's 'De Condicionibus Agrorum' is a technical treatise on land surveying and boundary laws, part of the Roman land-surveying (agrimensores) corpus. It is an important source for Roman land management and legal geography, studied by specialists in Roman law and surveying.

title: grammatica, fragmenta
author: Sinnius Capito
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Latin language--Grammar--Early works to 1500; perseus_author=Sinnius Capito active 1st century B.C.-1st century A.D; perseus_editor=Funaioli, Gino; perseus_year_published=1907; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: The grammatical fragments of Sinnius Capito, preserved by Funaioli, are scanty remains of early Latin grammatical thought. The generic title 'grammatica, fragmenta' indicates that the work's exact scope is uncertain, but it likely dealt with Latin language questions. Useful only for specialists in ancient linguistics.

title: grammatica
author: Staberius Eros
classification_notes: A tiny fragment of grammatical work by Staberius Eros, a freedman teacher known for his role in Brutus' education. The surviving text offers little content but marks an early phase of Latin grammatical study.

title: carmina
author: Sueius
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Sueius; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Sueius is known only from 111 words of his carmina (poems), which may have been on didactic or epic subjects (Morel classified him with epic poets). The fragments are too scant to characterize. The work is of interest only to specialists in fragmentary Latin poetry.

title: De Grammaticis et Rhetoribus
author: Suetonius
classification_notes: Suetonius' De Grammaticis et Rhetoribus is a biographical catalogue of Latin grammarians and rhetoricians. It is a key source for the history of Roman education and intellectual life.

title: De Historicis
author: Suetonius
classification_notes: Suetonius' De Historicis is a fragmentary work on Roman historians, known only through excerpts. It is a specialist text of interest to scholars of Suetonius and Roman historiography.

title: De Poetis
author: Suetonius
classification_notes: Suetonius' 'De Poetis' is a fragmentary work on the lives of Roman poets, part of his larger biographical corpus. It is an important source for the study of Latin literary history and Suetonius' biographical method.

title: De Vita Caesarum
author: Suetonius
classification_notes: Suetonius's De Vita Caesarum is a landmark of imperial biography, providing vivid and often scandalous portraits of Rome's first twelve Caesars.

title: Prata
author: Suetonius
classification_notes: Suetonius' Prata is a fragmentary encyclopedic work covering various topics, known only through excerpts. It is a specialist text of interest to scholars of Suetonius and Roman antiquarian learning.

title: fragmenta
author: Suetonius
classification_notes: Suetonius' fragmenta are unplaced fragments from his lost works. The generic title and brevity make classification uncertain; the work is of specialist interest for Suetonian scholarship and Latin biography.

title: oratio
author: T. Annius Luscus
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Speeches, addresses, etc., Latin; perseus_author=Annius Luscus, T active 156 B.C.-133 B.C; perseus_editor=Meyer, Heinrich; perseus_year_published=1842; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: A short oration fragment (13 words) attributed to T. Annius Luscus, a second-century BCE Roman politician and orator. The text survives only in grammatical citations and is of interest primarily for its evidence of early Latin oratory.

title: Eclogae
author: T. Calpurnius Siculus
classification_notes: The Eclogues of T. Calpurnius Siculus are a set of pastoral poems written in the Neronian period, imitating Virgil's Eclogues. They are a key text in the Latin pastoral tradition and offer insights into Neronian literary culture and imperial ideology. While not as widely read as Virgil, they are a common text for specialists in Latin pastoral and Neronian literature.

title: oratio
author: T. Labienus
classification_notes: A 21-word fragment of a speech by T. Labienus, an orator of the Augustan period. The brevity of the fragment limits analysis of his rhetorical methods.

title: epigramma
author: T. Quinctius Atta
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Atta, Titus Quinctius d. 77 B.C; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: A 6-word epigram attributed to the Roman playwright T. Quinctius Atta. This fragment is a minimal remnant of his poetic output, of interest only to specialists in fragmentary Republican literature.

title: togatae
author: T. Quinctius Atta
classification_notes: Fragments of togatae (Roman comedies in Italian dress) by Atta, preserved by later grammarians. The sparse remains offer limited insight into this subgenre of Latin comedy and are of interest only to specialists in Republican drama.

title: Tabulae Censoriae
author: Tabulae Censoriae
classification_notes: The Tabulae Censoriae are records of Roman censorial activities, preserved in fragmentary form. They provide primary evidence for Roman administrative and fiscal history, though their brevity limits interpretation.

title: De Disciplina Etrusca, frr.
author: Tarquitius Priscus
classification_notes: Tarquitius Priscus' De Disciplina Etrusca survives only in fragments, treating Etruscan religious and divinatory lore. It is an obscure specialist text, of interest primarily to historians of Roman religion and Etruscology.

title: Adelphoe
author: Terence
classification_notes: Terence's Adelphoe is a Roman comedy exploring themes of fatherhood and education, widely read and performed. It is a canonical work of Latin drama, frequently studied in curricula and scholarship.

title: Andria
author: Terence
classification_notes: Terence's 'Andria' is his first play and a foundational work of Roman comedy. It is central to the study of Latin drama, the reception of Greek New Comedy, and the history of Western theatre.

title: Eunuchus
author: Terence
classification_notes: Terence's Eunuchus is one of his most popular comedies, known for its lively characters and double plot. It is a staple of Latin literature and Roman drama studies.

title: Heauton Timorumenos
author: Terence
classification_notes: Terence's 'Heauton Timorumenos' (The Self-Tormentor) is a key Roman comedy, widely taught and performed for its refined language and moral themes. It is a canonical work of Latin dramatic literature.

title: Hecyra
author: Terence
classification_notes: Terence's Hecyra (The Mother-in-Law) is a Roman comedy with a complex plot of misunderstandings and reconciliation. It was Terence's least successful play in antiquity but is now studied for its sophisticated characterisation and structure. It is a common text in Latin literature courses.

title: Phormio
author: Terence
classification_notes: Terence's Phormio is a classic Roman comedy (fabula palliata) known for its intricate plot and character development. It is widely read and performed, a staple of Latin literature curricula.

title: De Litt., De Syll., De Metr.
author: Terentianus Maurus
classification_notes: Terentianus Maurus's treatises on letters, syllables, and meters are important testimonies to late Latin metrics and grammar, influential on medieval education.

title: Punica
author: Tiberius Catius Silius Italicus
classification_notes: Silius Italicus's 'Punica' is an epic poem on the Second Punic War, following the tradition of Virgil. It is the longest surviving Latin epic and a significant work of Flavian literature.

title: Elegiae
author: Tibullus
classification_notes: Tibullus's elegies, a major collection of Augustan love poetry.

title: carmina Tibulliana sp.
author: Tibullus
classification_notes: The Carmina Tibulliana (including poems attributed to Tibullus and others in the corpus Tibullianum) are a major collection of Latin elegy. They are central to the study of Augustan love poetry.

title: carmina
author: Ticidas
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Ticidas; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Minute fragments of Ticidas' poetry, a Neoteric poet of the late Republic, preserved only in later citations. The scant remains offer little to reconstruct his work and are of interest solely to specialists in Latin lyric and fragmentary verse.

title: togatae
author: Titinius
classification_notes: Togatae are Roman comedies set in a Roman (rather than Greek) context, by Titinius. These fragments are of specialist interest within Latin drama, illustrating an indigenous comic tradition.

title: grammatica
author: Titius
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Latin language--Grammar--Early works to 1500; perseus_author=Titius; perseus_editor=Funaioli, Gino; perseus_year_published=1907; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Attributed to the otherwise obscure grammarian Titius, this fragmentary text (seven words) is preserved in later grammatical compilations. Its content is too brief to assess except as a minor witness to early Latin grammatical study.

title: A.U.C. Perioch. ex P.Oxy.668
author: Titus Livius (Livy)
classification_notes: A fragment of the Periochae (summaries) of Livy's history, preserved on a papyrus from Oxyrhynchus. It offers a glimpse into the epitomizing tradition and is important for textual criticism of Livy.

title: Ab Urbe Condita
author: Titus Livius (Livy)
classification_notes: Livy's Ab Urbe Condita is the monumental history of Rome from its foundation, a cornerstone of Latin historiography. It is a canonical text, widely read and studied for its narrative artistry and historical content, and remains essential for Roman history and Latin literature.

title: Periochae Librorum A. U. C.
author: Titus Livius (Livy)
classification_notes: The Periochae of Livy's Ab Urbe Condita are summaries of the lost books of Livy's history. They are an essential resource for reconstructing Livy's narrative and for Roman historical studies.

title: fragmenta
author: Titus Livius (Livy)
classification_notes: Fragments of Livy's lost books, offering glimpses into his monumental history of Rome.

title: Amphitruo
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Amphitruo is a comedy by Plautus with a mythological theme. It is widely read and performed, a canonical work in Latin drama.

title: Asinaria
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Asinaria, a comedy of fraud and love, a representative work of Roman comic drama.

title: Aulularia
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Aulularia is a classic Roman comedy centered on the miser Euclio and his hidden pot of gold. It is one of Plautus's most famous plays, known for its lively characters and influence on later European drama.

title: Bacchides
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Bacchides, a Roman comedy based on a lost Greek original by Menander. Known for its intricate plot of mistaken identity and clever slaves, it is a key work in the corpus of Roman comic drama.

title: Captivi
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus' Captivi, a comedy about mistaken identity and loyalty, is notable for its moral tone among Plautus' works. It is a major play in the Plautine corpus and widely taught.

title: Casina
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus' Casina is a Roman comedy of mistaken identity and clever slaves, a staple of Latin literature. It is widely read and performed, representing the pinnacle of Roman comic drama.

title: Cistellaria
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's 'Cistellaria' is a Roman comedy, a representative work of the genre. It is a major work within the Plautine corpus, studied for its plot, characters, and use of language.

title: Curculio
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Curculio, a comedy of mistaken identity and clever schemes, a key work of Roman comic drama.

title: Epidicus
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Epidicus is a Roman comedy involving mistaken identity and clever slaves, showcasing Plautine humor and plot construction.

title: Fragmenta
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: These fragmenta of Plautus consist of surviving excerpts from lost plays. They are important for reconstructing the full scope of Plautus's work and are of common interest within Latin drama studies.

title: Menaechmi
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Menaechmi is one of Plautus's best-known comedies, the source for Shakespeare's Comedy of Errors. Its enduring influence and frequent study place it among the canonical works of Latin drama.

title: Mercator
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Mercator is a Roman comedy by Plautus, based on a Greek original. It is a staple of Latin literature curricula and widely studied for its linguistic and dramatic features, earning a major popularity rating within the Latin corpus.

title: Miles Gloriosus
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus' Miles Gloriosus is a Roman comedy centered on a boastful soldier, widely read and performed. It is a canonical work of Latin drama, frequently studied for its plot, characters, and linguistic humor.

title: Mostellaria
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Mostellaria, a comedy about a haunted house and a clever slave. It is among the most popular of Plautus's plays, influencing later European comedy.

title: Persa
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus' Persa is a Roman comedy of intrigue and mistaken identity, centered on a clever slave who outwits a pimp. It is a significant example of palliata comedy and is widely read for its lively dialogue, stock characters, and social satire.

title: Poenulus
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus' Poenulus (The Little Carthaginian) is a Roman comedy revolving around a Carthaginian father's search for his lost daughters. The play is notable for its sympathetic portrayal of a non-Roman character and its Punic-language passages. It is a commonly read and performed comedy, widely studied in Latin literature courses and by scholars of Roman theater.

title: Pseudolus
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus' Pseudolus, a Roman comedy of mistaken identity and clever servants, is among his most famous plays. It is widely studied in Latin literature courses and is a major work of Republican drama.

title: Rudens
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus' Rudens is a well-known Roman comedy featuring shipwreck, recognition, and a clever slave. It is a major work of Roman drama, frequently performed and studied for its lively dialogue and engagement with Greek New Comedy models.

title: Stichus
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Stichus is a Roman comedy about a pair of brothers and their resourceful slaves. While not among the most famous of Plautus's plays, it is well-known and studied for its plot, characters, and its reflection of Roman social life. It holds a major place within the corpus of Roman comedy.

title: Trinummus
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Trinummus ('The Three-Coined Man') is one of his most well-known comedies, a play about trust, friendship, and financial morality. As a major work of Roman comedy, it is frequently studied for its plot, characters, and its place in the Plautine corpus, as well as its influence on later European drama.

title: Truculentus
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus's Truculentus is a lively Roman comedy featuring a clever courtesan and rustic characters, exemplifying Plautus's farcical style and influence on later drama.

title: Vidularia
author: Titus Maccius Plautus
classification_notes: Plautus' Vidularia, a fragmentary comedy of which only about 1000 words survive, is known from a palimpsest. It is of specialist interest for Plautine textual criticism and plot reconstruction.

title: palliatae
author: Trabea
classification_notes: The palliatae (comedies in Greek dress) of Trabea, a playwright of the Republican period. Only 48 words survive, making this an obscure fragmentary text. These scant remains are of interest only to specialists in Roman comedy and the fragmentary tradition, providing minimal evidence of the work of a minor dramatist from the generation after Plautus.

title: epigramma in Ciceronis obitum
author: Tullius Laurea
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Laurea, Tullius 1st cent. B.C; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: An epigram on the death of Cicero by Tullius Laurea, a freedman of Cicero and minor poet.

title: satura
author: Turnus
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Turnus; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: A twelve-word fragment of satire (satura) by the obscure poet Turnus. Only a brief excerpt survives, preserving little of its original context.

title: de re rustica scripta
author: Turranius Niger
classification_notes: Turranius Niger's 'de re rustica scripta' is a fragmentary work on agriculture, known only from citations. It is a specialist text for the study of Roman agronomy and technical writing.

title: carmen
author: Vagellius
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Vagellius 1. Jh. n. Chr; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: A 17-word fragment of a Latin carmen by Vagellius, preserved from Morel's collection of early imperial fragments. The brevity and lack of context limit interpretation, but the fragment is associated with the epic tradition.

title: comoedia
author: Valerius
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Latin language--Grammar--Early works to 1500; perseus_author=Valerius, L fl. 53 B.C; perseus_editor=Funaioli, Gino; perseus_year_published=1907; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Valerius's comedy survives only in a nine-word fragment. Nothing else is known about the play or the author, making it a text of negligible scholarly reach.

title: epigrammata
author: Valerius Aedituus
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Valerius Aedituus fl. ca. 100 B.C; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Epigrams of Valerius Aedituus, early examples of Latin epigrammatic poetry.

title: Annales
author: Valerius Antias
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Latin language--Grammar--Early works to 1500; perseus_author=Valerius Antias, Quintus 1st cent. B.C; perseus_editor=Funaioli, Gino; perseus_year_published=1907; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Fragments of Valerius Antias' Annales, a historical work on early Rome. The text survives only in quotations and is used for the study of Roman historiography and the annalistic tradition.

title: Facta et Dicta Memorabilia
author: Valerius Maximus
classification_notes: Valerius Maximus's Facta et Dicta Memorabilia is a collection of memorable deeds and sayings from Roman and foreign history, arranged thematically. It was widely used as a source of exempla for rhetorical education and moral instruction.

title: De Orthographia
author: Velius Longus
classification_notes: Velius Longus's 'De Orthographia' is a Latin grammatical treatise on spelling and orthography. It is an important source for the history of Latin spelling and the Roman grammatical tradition.

title: Historia Romana
author: Velleius Paterculus
classification_notes: Velleius Paterculus' Historia Romana is a concise Roman history from the Trojan War to 30 CE. It is a valuable source for the Augustan and Tiberian periods, widely used by historians.

title: libri de rebus sacris
author: Veranius
classification_notes: Veranius' 'libri de rebus sacris' is a fragmentary work on Roman sacred law and ritual, preserved in brief quotations. It is a specialist source for the study of Roman religion.

title: Vita Iuvenalis
author: Vita Iuvenalis
classification_notes: Vita Iuvenalis is a short biography of the satirist Juvenal.

title: De Architectura
author: Vitruvius Pollio
classification_notes: Vitruvius's De Architectura is the sole surviving treatise on classical architecture from antiquity, covering engineering, town planning, and architectural theory, and has been enormously influential.

title: carmina
author: Volcacius Sedigitus
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin | Latin language--Grammar--Early works to 1500; perseus_author=Volcacius Sedigitus fl. 100 B.C; perseus_editor=Funaioli, Gino | Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1907 | 1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Volcacius Sedigitus' 'carmina' are fragmentary poems, known only from citations. The author is a minor figure, and the work is of interest primarily to specialists in Republican poetry and literary history.

title: carmen
author: Volumnius
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Volumnius; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: A four-word fragment of a carmen by Volumnius, an otherwise obscure Latin poet. The text is preserved only in later grammatical works and remains of minimal scholarly significance.

title: Tractatus
author: Zeno of Verona
classification_notes: Zeno of Verona's Tractatus, a collection of homilies on Christian doctrine and moral life. An important witness to early Latin patristics and North Italian theology.

title: epistula, fragmenta
author: mater Gracchorum Cornelia
classification_notes: Cornelia, mother of the Gracchi, is known from fragments of her letters (255 words). These are among the earliest Latin letters by a woman. They are historically significant for insights into Roman politics and family. However, the text is fragmentary and largely known through quotations, making it a specialist text.

title: Dubius Sermo
author: the Elder Pliny
classification_notes: Pliny the Elder's Dubius Sermo, a grammatical treatise on linguistic ambiguity, survives only in fragments. It is a specialist work important for the history of Latin grammatical theory and for understanding Pliny's philological interests.

title: Naturalis Historia
author: the Elder Pliny
classification_notes: Pliny the Elder's Naturalis Historia is a monumental encyclopedia of natural science, geography, and technology, unparalleled in Latin literature for its scope and detail. It remains a key source for Roman scientific knowledge.

title: Epistulae
author: the Younger Pliny
classification_notes: Pliny the Younger's Epistulae are a major collection of literary letters covering life in the early Roman Empire. They are invaluable for social history, politics, and the famous account of Vesuvius.

title: Panegyricus
author: the Younger Pliny
classification_notes: Pliny the Younger's Panegyricus is a lengthy speech of praise for Emperor Trajan, showcasing imperial oratory and serving as a model for later encomiastic rhetoric.

title: versus
author: the Younger Pliny
source_metadata_summary: perseus_subject=Epic poetry, Latin; perseus_author=Pliny the Younger; perseus_editor=Morel, Willy; perseus_year_published=1927; perseus_language=Latin
classification_notes: Only 131 words survive of verse by the Younger Pliny, known primarily for his letters and panegyric. These poetic fragments are slight and likely occasional. They are of specialist interest for the study of Pliny's literary output and Latin poetry in the imperial period.
